effort_complexity.rationale: Known bounded vendor, pin, recovery and public-exit repairs with deterministic fault injection.

release_benefit: Establishes the atomic bootstrap boundary required before exact Baton v0.15 content can replace the current pin.

design_decisions.choice: Bootstrap the conformance release under exact v0.15 records before its conformant Sworn engine exists.
options: staged manual v0.15 bootstrap with later engine revalidation
out-of-band implementation followed by retrospective planning
start under v0.13 and migrate executed records
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-15: use the staged manual v0.15 bootstrap and require S13 engine revalidation before automated authority.
design_decisions.rationale: This preserves specification-first delivery without inventing lifecycle evidence during the self-hosting transition.

positive_scenarios: The public command returns 0 for a clean check, 1 for deterministic drift, and 2 for invalid or operational failure without check-mode mutation.
An upstream write constructs VERSION bytes from one captured invocation instant and commits them in the same fully restored-or-applied transaction as every mapped vendor destination.
negative_scenarios: Preflight leaves the worktree unchanged; recoverable apply failure restores every mapped destination and VERSION exactly; incomplete rollback is explicit and blocks later success.
A fresh invocation rejects traversing, foreign, missing, symlinked or tampered Git-admin recovery material with exit 2, preserves recovery-only authority, and performs no ordinary vendor write.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: An atomic, exact preflight prevents protocol upgrades from manufacturing mixed-version repository state.
